summary: Verification attempt 2 passed. Spexus current_context was read before task access, task PERSONAL-TASK-1158 was read, all listed dependency and post-fix reports were present, and the regenerated post-fix screenshots were inspected directly. The focused fix in src/ui/render.ts, src/styles.css, and src/ui/render.test.ts adds labeled deal score-change cells and a stacked row-card layout for narrower desktop/laptop widths. The prior 1024x768 settlement clipping is no longer present: settlement laptop values remain inside the result panel horizontally, desktop settlement remains fully readable, ongoing score cards remain readable at desktop and laptop widths, and final desktop/laptop states show readable final ranking/settlement content with no continuation buttons. Release readiness is supported by the orchestrator post-fix rerun recording npm run verify passing with 13 test files, 126 tests, and a successful production build; a fresh local rerun was not needed because the post-fix rerun evidence and regenerated artifacts were complete and current.

tests_run.evidence: Dependency report present with status succeeded, empty risks, and failure_type not_applicable.
Dependency report present with status succeeded, empty risks, and npm run verify evidence for 120 tests plus build.
Dependency report present with status succeeded, empty risks, UI score/result implementation evidence, and live browser capture evidence.
Dependency report present with status succeeded, empty risks, and US-844 integration coverage evidence.
Dependency report present with status succeeded, empty risks, and accessibility/release readiness evidence.
Attempt 1 failure was confirmed as nfr_gap caused by right-edge clipping of output/playwright/mz-406-settlement-laptop.png at 1024x768.

Reviewed the focused fix: render.ts adds data-cell-label values for Было/Изменение/Стало cells, styles.css stacks result table rows below 1180px with visible labels, and render.test.ts asserts the narrow-layout labels plus no finished-state continuation controls.

Post-fix rerun recorded targeted UI/integration tests passing, local Vite capture passing, Playwright metrics with horizontalOverflow=false and no overflowingElements for all states, final_state_buttons=[], and npm run verify passing with 13 test files, 126 tests, tsc, and vite build.

tests_run.command: manual inspection of output/playwright/mz-406-active-desktop.png, mz-406-settlement-desktop.png, mz-406-final-desktop.png, mz-406-active-laptop.png, mz-406-settlement-laptop.png, mz-406-final-laptop.png
tests_run.evidence: Desktop and laptop active score cards are readable; desktop settlement table is fully visible; laptop settlement no longer clips score/result columns horizontally and uses labeled stacked cells; desktop and laptop final result states are readable and show no continuation buttons.
Confirmed desktop screenshots are 1280x800 and laptop screenshots are 1024x768.

verification_environment.fresh_verify_rerun_reason: The orchestrator post-fix visual rerun already supplied current regenerated screenshots, Playwright overflow metrics, final-state button metrics, and npm run verify evidence after the focused fix.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Ongoing score display exposes bullet, mountain, and mutual-whist values for all three players across desktop and laptop widths.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Visual evidence uses deterministic synthetic score fixtures rendered through the app capture helper; full mobile is explicitly out of scope.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Deal settlement and final result surfaces show readable summaries, score changes, ranking, settlement text, and correct controls.
evidence_matrix.limitations: No pixel-diff baseline exists; verification is direct visual inspection plus post-fix Playwright overflow/button metrics.

evidence_matrix.scenario: After a deal completes, the score table/result display shows updated bullet, mountain, and mutual-whist before/delta/after values without clipped columns.
evidence_matrix.limitations: At 1024x768 the table intentionally stacks into row cards; this preserves labeled before/delta/after values and removes the prior horizontal clipping.

evidence_matrix.scenario: When the bullet target is reached, the final result/ranking is shown and accidental continuation is prevented.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Verified for desktop/laptop final screenshots and DOM/control evidence; full mobile is out of scope.

evidence_matrix.scenario: The verification task confirms the score/result UI implemented by MZ-403 has no clipped score/result columns or incoherent overlap in desktop/laptop scope.
evidence_matrix.limitations: The original laptop settlement horizontal clipping from attempt 1 is resolved in regenerated post-fix artifacts.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Regression/release readiness is supported after the focused visual fix.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Verifier did not rerun npm run verify locally because current orchestrator post-fix evidence was sufficient and complete.

quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Desktop/laptop only; full mobile out of scope.
Laptop uses stacked row cards instead of a wide table; this is the intended post-fix layout.
No pixel-diff baseline exists; direct screenshot inspection and DOM/button metrics were used.
Validated 1280x800 desktop and 1024x768 laptop artifacts; full mobile intentionally excluded by task scope.
Relies on orchestrator post-fix rerun for npm run verify rather than a duplicate local rerun in this verifier attempt.
This attempt focused on visual regression; accessibility was covered by dependency verification and targeted render assertions for labels/no continuation.
Visual regression task with no security-sensitive behavior or data boundary changes.
